Genes.Breeding_or_Biofortification_Value: Biofortification; essential component of the minimal gene set for rose terpenoid engineering in rice endosperm; rate-limiting step that must be overexpressed to supply precursors; future improvement suggested by plastid-targeting of HMGS and GPPS
Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect_on_Nutrient: OsDXS2 overexpression in combination with RcG/FPPS1 and ObGES (DPG lines) enabled de novo production of rose terpenoids (up to 1740 μg/kg FW) in rice endosperm; DH lines (OsDXS2+AtHMGS) increased IPP/DMAPP levels but did not produce rose terpenoids without downstream enzymes
Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Endosperm-specific overexpression in stable transgenic rice (T0, T1, T2 generations); RT-qPCR confirmation of transgene expression (up to 15-fold higher than ZH11); GC-MS metabolite quantification; transient co-expression in N. benthamiana leaves
Genes.Enzyme_Name_or_Function: 1-deoxy-D-xylulose-5-phosphate synthase 2; rate-limiting enzyme of the plastidial MEP pathway synthesizing IPP/DMAPP precursors
Genes.Experimental_Materials: Stable transgenic overexpression lines DH (OsDXS2+AtHMGS) and DPG (OsDXS2+RcG/FPPS1+ObGES) in ZH11 background; transient expression in N. benthamiana
Genes.Expression_Pattern: Low endogenous expression in rice seeds/endosperm (identified as bottleneck); transgenic overexpression driven by endosperm-specific promoters (GluA2, GluB4, GluC)
Genes.Field_Trials_or_GxE: Field trials conducted in Yazhou District, Hainan, China (109°17'E, 18°35'N); replicated paddy field trials with three biological replicates per genotype
Genes.Final_Nutrient_Product: Rose terpenoids (geraniol, linalool, α-terpineol, β-myrcene, trans-β-ionone, β-damascenone)
Genes.Flux_or_Direction_Change: Overexpression increases flux through MEP pathway toward IPP/DMAPP, providing universal terpenoid precursors; upregulates downstream terpenoid pathway genes in transgenic endosperm
Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Candidate gene approach based on pathway analysis; low expression of OsDXS2 in rice seeds identified as bottleneck via transcriptional profiling
Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Standard paddy field conditions; sensory evaluation with TPS buffer treatment at 75°C
Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: OsDXS2 is part of a minimal three-gene combination (OsDXS2+RcG/FPPS1+ObGES) identified through combinatorial screening in tobacco. The three-gene system performed as effectively as a five-gene system for rose terpenoid production.
Genes.Nutrient_Assay_or_Profiling_Method: GC-MS (HS-SPME-GC-MS volatilome profiling); DPPH radical scavenging assay for antioxidant capacity
Genes.Omics_Data: Volatilome profiling (GC-MS); RT-qPCR expression profiling of MEP and MVA pathway genes; single-cell transcriptomic data from REGSTA database (Stereo-seq of rice embryo)
Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: No significant differences in key agronomic traits (plant height, flag leaf dimensions, 1000-grain weight, grain morphology) compared to ZH11; enhanced antioxidant activity (DPPH scavenging)
Genes.Pathway_Name: Methylerythritol phosphate (MEP) pathway; rose terpenoid biosynthesis
Genes.Quantitative_Nutrient_Change: Up to 1740 μg/kg FW rose terpenoids in DPG lines; increased IPP/DMAPP levels in both DH and DPG lines relative to ZH11
Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: Transcriptional overexpression driven by endosperm-specific promoters; upregulated downstream MEP pathway gene expression in DPG lines
Genes.Regulatory_Relationships: OsDXS2->increases flux->IPP/DMAPP; OsDXS2 OE->upregulates->MEP pathway genes in endosperm
Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsDXS2 encodes the rate-limiting enzyme of the MEP pathway and has low endogenous expression in rice seeds, making it a key bottleneck for terpenoid precursor supply. 2. Overexpression of OsDXS2 in combination with RcG/FPPS1 and ObGES (DPG lines) enabled de novo rose terpenoid production (up to 1740 μg/kg FW) in rice endosperm. 3. OsDXS2 overexpression alone (in DH lines with AtHMGS) increased IPP/DMAPP levels but was insufficient for rose terpenoid production without downstream pathway enzymes. 4. DPG transgenic lines showed universally upregulated flux through the rose terpenoid biosynthetic pathway. 5. No agronomic yield penalty was observed in field trials.

Genes.Breeding_or_Biofortification_Value: Biofortification; essential for GPP supply in the minimal gene set for rose terpenoid engineering; bifunctionality eliminates need for separate GPPS and FPPS genes; future improvement suggested by plastid-targeting to amplify expression
Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect_on_Nutrient: Essential component of the minimal gene set; co-expression with OsDXS2 and ObGES enabled de novo production of rose terpenoids in rice endosperm (up to 1740 μg/kg FW) and tobacco leaves (up to 2541 μg/kg FW)
Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient co-expression in N. benthamiana leaves; stable endosperm-specific overexpression in transgenic rice (DPG lines); RT-qPCR confirmation; GC-MS metabolite quantification
Genes.Enzyme_Name_or_Function: Bifunctional geranyl diphosphate/farnesyl diphosphate synthase; synthesizes both GPP and FPP from IPP and DMAPP
Genes.Experimental_Materials: Transient expression via Agrobacterium co-infiltration in N. benthamiana (pEAQ-HT-DEST2 vector); stable transgenic DPG lines in rice ZH11 (GoldenBraid 2.0 system)
Genes.Expression_Pattern: Endosperm-specific expression driven by GluB4 promoter in transgenic rice; confirmed by RT-qPCR in T0, T1, T2 endosperms
Genes.Field_Trials_or_GxE: Field trials in Yazhou District, Hainan, China
Genes.Final_Nutrient_Product: Rose terpenoids (geraniol, linalool, α-terpineol, β-myrcene, trans-β-ionone, β-damascenone)
Genes.Flux_or_Direction_Change: Channels IPP/DMAPP toward GPP (monoterpenoid precursor) and FPP (sesquiterpenoid precursor); bifunctionality provides both C10 and C15 prenyl diphosphate substrates for downstream terpenoid synthases
Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Candidate gene from Rosa chinensis terpenoid pathway; selected based on its bifunctional GPP/FPP synthase activity
Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: The bifunctionality of RcG/FPPS1 contrasts with tea (Camellia sinensis), which relies on two separate enzymes (CsNudix26 and CsTPS1). This bifunctionality simplifies the engineering strategy.
Genes.Nutrient_Assay_or_Profiling_Method: GC-MS (HS-SPME-GC-MS); calibration curves with authentic standards for six rose terpenoids
Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: No agronomic yield penalty in DPG rice lines; enhanced antioxidant capacity
Genes.Pathway_Name: Terpenoid backbone biosynthesis; rose terpenoid biosynthesis pathway
Genes.Quantitative_Nutrient_Change: Up to 2541 μg/kg FW in tobacco; up to 1740 μg/kg FW in rice endosperm
Genes.Regulatory_Relationships: OsDXS2->provides IPP/DMAPP->RcG/FPPS1->produces GPP->ObGES->produces geraniol and downstream rose terpenoids
Genes.Species: Rose (heterologously expressed in Rice and Tobacco)
Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RcG/FPPS1 from Rosa chinensis is a bifunctional enzyme that synthesizes both GPP and FPP, providing key terpenoid precursors. 2. It is an essential component of the minimal three-gene set (OsDXS2+RcG/FPPS1+ObGES) for rose terpenoid production. 3. Co-expression with ObGES is required for geraniol synthesis, as RcG/FPPS1 provides the GPP substrate. 4. Rose terpenoids were exclusively detected in DPG lines containing this gene, not in DH lines lacking it. 5. Single- and double-gene constructs without the complete pathway failed to produce most rose terpenoids.
Genes.Units_and_Basis: μg/kg FW, grain/endosperm and leaf tissue

Genes.Breeding_or_Biofortification_Value: Biofortification; essential terminal enzyme for geraniol and downstream rose terpenoid production; part of the minimal gene set for aroma biofortification of rice
Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect_on_Nutrient: Essential terminal enzyme for rose terpenoid production; geraniol synthesis depends on RcG/FPPS1+ObGES co-expression; DPG lines containing ObGES exclusively produced rose terpenoids while DH lines lacking it did not
Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient co-expression in N. benthamiana leaves (16 combinatorial constructs); stable endosperm-specific overexpression in transgenic rice (DPG lines); RT-qPCR; GC-MS metabolite quantification with standards
Genes.Enzyme_Name_or_Function: Geraniol synthase; converts GPP to geraniol, the key monoterpene alcohol precursor of rose terpenoids
Genes.Experimental_Materials: Transient expression in N. benthamiana (pEAQ-HT-DEST2); stable transgenic DPG lines in rice ZH11 (GoldenBraid 2.0)
Genes.Expression_Pattern: Endosperm-specific expression driven by GluC promoter in transgenic rice; confirmed by RT-qPCR
Genes.Field_Trials_or_GxE: Field trials in Yazhou District, Hainan, China
Genes.Final_Nutrient_Product: Rose terpenoids (geraniol, linalool, α-terpineol, β-myrcene, trans-β-ionone, β-damascenone)
Genes.Flux_or_Direction_Change: Converts GPP to geraniol, the gateway monoterpene for rose aroma compounds; geraniol is further converted to linalool, α-terpineol, and other rose terpenoids
Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Candidate gene from Ocimum basilicum; known geraniol synthase (Iijima et al., 2004)
Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Sensory evaluation with TPS buffer treatment at 75°C to release volatile aromas
Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: ObGES is from sweet basil (Ocimum basilicum), not from rose itself. The combinatorial screening in tobacco identified that this gene in combination with RcG/FPPS1 and OsDXS2 constitutes the minimal gene set, making additional genes (RhNUDX1, AmGPP.SSU, AtHMGS) dispensable for rose terpenoid production.
Genes.Nutrient_Assay_or_Profiling_Method: GC-MS (HS-SPME-GC-MS); quantification using authentic standards with calibration curves
Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: Floral aroma detected by sensory panel; enhanced antioxidant activity; no agronomic yield penalty
Genes.Pathway_Name: Monoterpenoid biosynthesis; rose terpenoid biosynthesis pathway
Genes.Quantitative_Nutrient_Change: Up to 2541 μg/kg FW total rose terpenoids in tobacco; up to 1740 μg/kg FW in rice endosperm (DPG lines)
Genes.Species: Sweet basil (heterologously expressed in Rice and Tobacco)
Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ObGES from Ocimum basilicum encodes geraniol synthase, the terminal enzyme converting GPP to geraniol. 2. Geraniol synthesis depends on co-expression of RcG/FPPS1+ObGES, defining the minimal downstream gene requirement. 3. Rose terpenoids were exclusively detected in lines expressing ObGES (DPG lines), confirming its essential role. 4. The three-gene combination (OsDXS2+RcG/FPPS1+ObGES) performed as effectively as a five-gene system. 5. DPG rice grains exhibited floral aroma in sensory evaluation and enhanced antioxidant capacity.
Genes.Units_and_Basis: μg/kg FW, grain/endosperm and leaf tissue

Genes.Breeding_or_Biofortification_Value: Biofortification; boosts terpenoid precursor (IPP/DMAPP) supply via MVA pathway; identified as likely rate-limiting enzyme; future improvement suggested by plastid-targeting to amplify expression and bypass compartmentation
Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect_on_Nutrient: Co-expression with OsDXS2 in DH lines increased IPP/DMAPP levels and enhanced antioxidant activity, but did not produce rose terpenoids (requires downstream ObGES/RcG/FPPS1)
Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Stable endosperm-specific overexpression in transgenic rice (DH lines: OsDXS2+AtHMGS); RT-qPCR; GC-MS metabolite quantification; transient expression in N. benthamiana
Genes.Enzyme_Name_or_Function: 3-hydroxy-3-methylglutaryl-CoA synthase (HMGS); catalyzes condensation of acetoacetyl-CoA and acetyl-CoA to HMG-CoA in the cytosolic mevalonate (MVA) pathway
Genes.Experimental_Materials: Stable transgenic DH lines (OsDXS2+AtHMGS) in rice ZH11; transient expression in N. benthamiana
Genes.Expression_Pattern: Endosperm-specific expression driven by GluA2 promoter in transgenic rice; HMGS identified as likely rate-limiting enzyme in MVA pathway based on expression changes in DPG lines
Genes.Field_Trials_or_GxE: Field trials in Yazhou District, Hainan, China
Genes.Final_Nutrient_Product: IPP/DMAPP (terpenoid universal precursors); contributes to rose terpenoid precursor pool and antioxidant capacity
Genes.Flux_or_Direction_Change: Increases flux through the MVA pathway toward IPP/DMAPP; MVA pathway genes were significantly upregulated in DH lines; in DPG lines, only OsHMGS expression showed significant changes among MVA pathway genes, indicating HMGS as the likely rate-limiting enzyme
Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Candidate gene from Arabidopsis thaliana MVA pathway
Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: AtHMGS was part of the DH construct but not the minimal gene set (DPG) for rose terpenoid production. It contributes to precursor supply via the cytosolic MVA pathway, complementing the plastidial MEP pathway contribution of OsDXS2.
Genes.Nutrient_Assay_or_Profiling_Method: GC-MS volatilome profiling; DPPH radical scavenging assay
Genes.Omics_Data: Volatilome profiling; RT-qPCR expression analysis of MVA and MEP pathway genes
Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: Enhanced antioxidant activity in DH lines; no agronomic yield penalty
Genes.Quantitative_Nutrient_Change: Increased IPP/DMAPP levels relative to ZH11; enhanced DPPH radical scavenging activity in DH lines
Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: Transcriptional overexpression; upregulated MVA pathway genes in DH lines
Genes.Species: Arabidopsis (heterologously expressed in Rice and Tobacco)
Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtHMGS from Arabidopsis encodes HMG-CoA synthase, identified as the likely rate-limiting enzyme of the MVA pathway in rice endosperm. 2. DH lines (OsDXS2+AtHMGS) showed significantly upregulated MVA pathway genes and increased IPP/DMAPP levels. 3. DH lines enhanced antioxidant activity but did not produce rose terpenoids without the downstream minimal gene set. 4. In DPG lines, only OsHMGS expression showed significant changes among MVA pathway genes, further supporting HMGS as the key regulatory point. 5. Future strategy suggests plastid-targeting of HMGS to further boost terpenoid production.

Title: Combinatorial screening in tobacco enables engineering of rose-terpenoid-enriched rice endosperm
